summary: Added project instance tabs from Project Explorer for browsing reserving-class paths and project dataset types.
details: Project tree rows now reveal a red open-corner View project contents in a new tab icon on hover or keyboard focus, opening a top-level project instance tab titled with the project name.
Project instance tabs show a reserving-class path selector, a dataset type table, and floating in-tab dataset windows that reuse the existing Dataset Viewer page with project/path prefilled.
Opening a dataset that already has an open or hidden floating window activates or restores the existing window instead of creating a duplicate.
New floating dataset windows default to about 80% of the project instance frame and reuse the most recent floating dataset window size for subsequent opens.
Floating dataset window titlebars show the full reserving-class path plus dataset name while minimized toolbar tabs use the dataset name.
Floating dataset window titlebars include shell-matching minimize, maximize/restore, and close icon buttons.
Floating dataset windows inside project instance tabs use rounded frame corners when the shell's Windows 11 frame flag is active.
Floating dataset windows inside project instance tabs can be resized from all corners and edges, with the same dotted resize glyph as the main shell on the southeast handle.
Floating dataset windows inside project instance tabs can be maximized within the project-instance frame by double-clicking the titlebar, then restored by double-clicking or dragging the titlebar.
Floating dataset window titlebar double-clicks are handled before drag capture so maximize and restore reliably trigger.
Floating dataset windows inside project instance tabs can be dragged partially off the left, right, and bottom edges while keeping a side grab area and titlebar reachable.

Project instance tabs support Ctrl+W to close the active floating dataset window, including while focus is inside the embedded Dataset Viewer or when Electron routes the close-tab shortcut through the parent shell first.

Project instance toolbars include a hidden-tab collection area where floating dataset windows dragged by titlebar anywhere above the main layout highlight the dragged window, show a release-to-minimize banner, minimize with a slower dock-style animation into the matching large-radius minimized toolbar tab showing only the dataset name with a styled full-title hover tooltip, and can later be previewed by full window title from the hidden-tabs button with a one-second hover grace period, restored from the matching minimized tab, restored in bulk, closed individually, or closed in bulk from a wider dropdown list.

The reserving-class path selector embeds the same lazy picker body used elsewhere in the app, including the Shortcut section, hidden/filter preferences, favorites, user-defined favorite folders, shared icons, and independent scrolling.
The project instance path tree uses tighter left and right spacing with a small left inset and removes the two-sided scrollbar gutter so more path text fits in the left panel.

Project instance dataset tables now support a right-click Table View Settings window for grouping by any dataset column, rendering one table per distinct grouped value with record counts, and each table header supports filter dropdowns, drag-to-reorder columns, and drag-to-resize column widths.
Project instance tabs initialize the selected reserving-class path from the project's last-used path, falling back to the first Shortcut item when no last-used path is available.
Dataset viewer windows keep move and resize tracking smooth over embedded iframe content by adding a temporary drag shield.

The project instance toolbar uses a compact height, shows only the selected reserving-class path without a duplicate selected-path strip in the left panel, uses larger selected-path text for readability, and caps the path label width so more minimized tabs can fit on the right.
Project instance panel title bars are omitted so the tree and dataset table start directly below the toolbar.

The reserving-class panel defaults to 400px, resizes between 200px and 600px, animates collapse/expand, disables grid transitions and throttles width updates during live splitter drag for responsiveness, collapses when dragged to 200px or smaller, and toggles collapse/expand on splitter double-click.
